title: How the 'Project Hail Mary' book walks the line between hard and speculative science fiction… and why the film did not
summary: The article discusses the balance between hard and speculative science fiction in Andy Weir's 'Project Hail Mary' novel and compares it to the film adaptation, highlighting differences in storytelling approaches.

title: 'Tall waves moving in slow motion': Here's how oily oceans on Saturn's giant moon Titan may behave
summary: Researchers are studying how waves behave in the oily oceans of Saturn's moon Titan, considering both the liquid's properties and the moon's gravity. This study helps understand the dynamics of alien worlds' surfaces.

title: 'For All Mankind' alternative timeline vs reality: How Apple TV's sci-fi show diverges from history
summary: The article compares Apple TV's sci-fi series 'For All Mankind' with real historical space exploration over six decades. It highlights key divergences between the show's alternate timeline and actual events.

title: Hubble Space Telescope spots a mesmerizing spiral galaxy | Space photo of the day for April 17, 2026
summary: The Hubble Space Telescope captured a stunning image of a spiral galaxy, showcasing its ongoing contributions to astronomical discoveries. This latest photo highlights the telescope's enduring legacy in space science.

title: Artemis 2's heat shield seems to have aced its trial by fire
summary: Artemis 2's Orion capsule's heat shield successfully endured its re-entry trial through Earth's atmosphere. Mission commander Reid Wiseman confirmed the heat shield performed exceptionally well during the test.

title: Interstellar invader comet 3I/ATLAS made a startling transformation as it passed the sun
summary: The interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS underwent significant changes during its passage near the sun in fall 2023, according to astronomers' observations.

title: This giant telescope could discover habitable exoplanets and secrets of our universe — if it gets its funding
summary: The Giant Magellan Telescope in Chile is entering its final design phase before seeking funding from Congress. The project aims to discover habitable exoplanets and uncover secrets of the universe.

title: Landing astronauts on the moon 'is absolutely doable, and it's doable soon,' NASA's Artemis 2 commander says
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 commander stated that landing astronauts on the moon is feasible and imminent. The crew reported enhanced knowledge of the Orion capsule, which could benefit upcoming Artemis missions.

title: CSDA Quality Assessment Report Evaluates Satellogic NewSat Data
summary: The CSDA Quality Assessment Report has evaluated Satellogic's NewSat data, highlighting its contributions to Earth science research and applications. This adds to the growing evidence of commercial data's role in scientific advancements.

title: Webinar 4/29: NASA CSDA Program Vendor Focus- MDA Space
summary: NASA's CSDA program will host a vendor focus webinar on April 29 featuring MDA Space. The event aims to provide insights into the program's offerings.

title: NASA Artemis II Human Research Data Methodology Challenge
summary: NASA's Human Research Program (HRP) is developing methods to safeguard astronaut health for long-term missions, including Artemis II and future Mars exploration. The program utilizes ground research, the ISS, and analog environments to monitor human health in deep space.

title: NASA, OPM Announce New NASA Force Website, Open Job Applications
summary: NASA and the U.S. Office of Personnel Management launched the NASA Force website to open job applications for high-impact technical roles. The initiative aims to recruit top engineers and technologists to support NASA's exploration, research, and advanced technology priorities.

title: Quality Assessment Report Evaluates Tomorrow.io Precipitation Radar Data
summary: A quality assessment report has evaluated the precipitation radar data from Tomorrow.io, highlighting its contributions to Earth science research. The report underscores the growing role of commercial data in scientific applications.

title: Volunteers Discover Rare Space Weather Events Using Their Ears
summary: Volunteers are helping scientists analyze rare space weather events by listening to data sonified from NASA's HARP project, which compares Earth's magnetic field to a 'giant harp in space.' The HARP citizen science initiative transforms magnetic field data into audible sounds to study wave behavior.

title: NASA’s SPHEREx Observatory Maps Interstellar Ice in Milky Way
summary: NASA's SPHEREx Observatory has mapped interstellar ice and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons in the Milky Way's Cygnus X region. This discovery highlights the chemical complexity of star-forming areas.

title: NASA Invites Media to Latvia Artemis Accords Signing Ceremony
summary: Latvia will sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at NASA Headquarters in Washington on April 20. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman will host Latvian officials including Minister Dace Melbārde.

title: At the Edge of Light
summary: A photograph taken on April 6, 2026, captures the Moon's far side along the terminator, highlighting the Orientale Basin with long shadows from low-angle sunlight. The image reveals the basin's structure under these unique lighting conditions.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have completed full engine static fires as part of the Artemis program. This milestone follows the successful return of Orion.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew conducted a spacewalk and managed vehicle departures on the International Space Station in March. This marked another active month for the crew onboard the orbital laboratory.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will conduct its third launch with a previously flown booster. Six launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent Cygnus S.S. William McCool mission concluding its CRS NG-23 flight.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marks a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than any previous crewed mission. The mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has debuted new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making refinements at Starbase Pad 2 in preparation for the return of Booster 19. The updates are part of ongoing efforts to enhance launch infrastructure at the facility.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II set a record by conducting a lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft named Integrity, carrying four astronauts. This mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station, while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. The news highlights ongoing space missions alongside NASA's Artemis II mission.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but the specific details were not provided in the input.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of space exploration milestones.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to share feedback on space sector topics during the annual SME Forum 2026, held in Paris on June 8-9, 2026, with an online preparatory session in April-May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received at an optimal location. The achievement marks a significant milestone for the project.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has generated 57 artificial solar eclipses and collected over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data, revealing that solar wind structures move 3-4 times faster than previously thought.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved guidance or navigation systems. It highlights a breakthrough or innovation in this area.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels during ESA's parabolic flight campaign, achieving significant thrust in microgravity conditions. The study, led by ULB and Khalifa University, suggests potential applications for steering solar sails and satellite positioning.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Lasers could steer solar sails and adjust satellite positions in space using graphene. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated graphene's potential to revolutionize space propulsion.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A new silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space technology applications. This advancement could impact various sectors within the space industry.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Thruster Market with Gauss
summary: Rocket Lab is entering the thruster market with its new Gauss product line, aiming to address scalability concerns for customers. The company's CFO highlighted the need for vertical integration to meet high-volume production demands.

title: Axiom Space’s Suit Set to Fly in 2027
summary: Axiom Space is developing its first qualification spacesuit, with plans for it to fly in 2027. The company's chief astronaut confirmed that production is ongoing despite external factors.

title: Voyager Technologies Wins Its First Private ISS Mission
summary: Voyager Technologies has secured its first private mission to the International Space Station (ISS) in 2026, breaking Axiom Space's monopoly on NASA's PAM contracts. This marks a shift in the commercial spaceflight landscape with new competitors emerging.

title: Seraphim Space Aims To Shape Strategy With New Advisory Council
summary: Seraphim Space has formed a new advisory council to address key challenges in the space industry, including sustainability, regulatory issues, national security, and international collaboration.

title: ORBX ETF Launches Amid Wave of Space Tech IPOs
summary: ORBX ETF launches to track companies earning over half their revenue from space tech, amid a wave of space tech IPOs.

title: Kepler Leads Team to Test ESA’s ‘Fiber in the Sky’
summary: ESA has selected a team led by Kepler Communications to test the agency's 'fiber in the sky' HydRON orbital communications network. The initiative aims to advance high-speed space-based data transmission.

title: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman On Artemis, Budget, And Establishing a Lasting Space Vision
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman discusses the Artemis program, budget challenges, and the need for a sustainable space vision. He highlights the scientific potential of passionate teams but questions their financial planning expertise.

title: CLD Companies Say NASA Is Wrong. NASA Says Prove It.
summary: CLD companies challenge NASA's claims, while NASA invites them to disprove the statements. The dispute was highlighted during the Space Symposium.

title: Slingshot Introduces AI-Powered Portal Platform
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched an AI-powered platform to help operators detect anomalies and make decisions in a unified portal. The platform aims to streamline space operations through artificial intelligence.

title: ICON Prime Launches, Led By Former TX Rep
summary: ICON Prime has launched as a new business unit led by a former Texas representative, focusing on collaborations with the Pentagon and NASA. The robotic construction company ICON aims to expand its operations into government partnerships.

title: IFC News Roundup from AIX
summary: Satellite companies showcased in-flight connectivity (IFC) solutions at the AIX Aircraft Interiors Expo, aiming to attract airlines. The event highlighted significant announcements in the satellite industry.

title: Airlines See IFC as Way to Build Relationships With Customers
summary: Airlines are leveraging in-flight connectivity (IFC) to enhance customer relationships. The initiative is highlighted as a key part of the modern flying experience.

title: NorthStar Earth & Space to Go Public in SPAC Deal at $300M Valuation
summary: NorthStar Earth & Space plans to go public via a SPAC deal valued at $300 million. The Canadian space domain awareness company integrates ground-based sensors for tracking objects in orbit.

title: ESA Taps Kepler to Lead Next Stage of HydRON Optical Program
summary: The European Space Agency has selected Kepler Communications as the prime contractor for HydRON Element 3, the next phase of a sovereign optical communications constellation. This marks a significant step in advancing optical communication technology in space.

title: Space Force, NATO Commanders Share Threat Perspectives
summary: U.S. Space Force is actively identifying new space capabilities and managing threats from adversaries while creating missions to defend assets. NATO commanders share perspectives on space security threats.

title: Air Force Awarded Development Contract for Space-Based AMTI; Meink Says Risk Low
summary: The U.S. Department of the Air Force awarded a contract to multiple vendors for the development of space-based air moving target indication (AMTI). The initiative is noted for having low risk according to Meink.

title: Boeing and Millennium Space Systems Add Mid-Class Resolute Satellite Bus
summary: Boeing and its subsidiary Millennium Space Systems have introduced a new mid-class satellite platform called Resolute, offering enhanced capabilities compared to traditional small satellites. The Resolute satellite bus is designed to provide greater functionality for space missions.

title: Slingshot Aerospace Releases AI-Powered Analytics Platform for Space Operations
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched Portal, an AI-powered analytics platform for satellite data monitoring and analysis. The software integrates Slingshot’s data flows with external sources to enhance space operations.

title: Industry Builds the Case Around Architecture Options for Orbital Data Centers
summary: Reductions in satellite launch prices and demand for alternatives to terrestrial data centers are making orbital space data centers more appealing. The industry is exploring architecture options for these orbital facilities.

title: Transport Layer of PWSA Will Eventually Transition From SDA to New Acquisition Executive, Sandhoo Says
summary: The Space Force plans to transfer the communications layer of the Space Development Agency's Proliferated Warfighter Space Architecture (PWSA) to a new acquisition executive. This transition is part of the Space Force's broader acquisition structure changes.
